title: Severe headache and vision changes in a 48-year-old
presentation: A 48-year-old male with poorly controlled hypertension presents with a severe headache and blurry vision for 4 hours. He ran out of his amlodipine 2 weeks ago. Vitals: BP 218/136, HR 92, RR 16, Temp 37.1C, SpO2 98%. Exam: papilledema on fundoscopy, mild confusion, no focal neurological deficits, trace proteinuria on dipstick.

gold_standard_workup.key_decision_point: BP >180/120 + end organ damage (papilledema, confusion, proteinuria) = hypertensive emergency. Reduce MAP by no more than 25% in first hour. Do NOT drop BP too fast.
